other_observers: seen on 7/14 with Nick Komar Jr and Sr, and Cole Wild; first found here by Mark Peterson and Brandon Percival
description: the birds were about the size of Western Tanagers seen earlier in the day, and significantly larger than Yellow Warblers nearby. The bill on the one male I saw well was brownish yellow, and fairly large for a passerine, and especially so for a tanager.  The overall body color of both males I saw was intense bright red, with no other colors visible. The wings and tail were the same color.Behaviors: The birds were singing and calling from along the riparian corridor along Yellow Jacket creek, apparently defending two seperate territories.Call: Both males continuously uttered a loud and fairly unmusical "pitty-tuck" call, and were orignially tracked down by listening for this. At one point I heard two different birds at the same time singing a robin-like song, but quite fast, and with some burry-er phrases interspersed in between.Plumage: alternate
